_comment: PoE currency quick reference. Major orbs and currency items, what they do, and primary crafting uses.

currencies.name: Scroll of Wisdom
currencies.effect: Identifies an unidentified item.
currencies.primary_use: Identifying rare and unique items. Extremely common.
currencies.notes: Effectively zero value. You can vendor Portal Scrolls (5:1) for Wisdom Scrolls.

currencies.name: Portal Scroll
currencies.effect: Opens a portal to the current map's entrance (Town Portal).
currencies.primary_use: Town trips during mapping. Vendor recipe: 3× Portal Scrolls → 1 Transmutation.
currencies.notes: Always carry a stack. Early game use only — endgame players use Portal skill gems instead.

currencies.name: Orb of Transmutation
currencies.effect: Upgrades a Normal (white) item to Magic (blue), adding 1–2 modifiers.
currencies.primary_use: Crafting magic flasks; rolling magic maps for Sextants.
currencies.notes: Abundant. Used in quantity for flask crafting early game.

currencies.name: Orb of Augmentation
currencies.effect: Adds a modifier to a Magic item that has only one modifier.
currencies.primary_use: Adding a second affix to a magic item before using an Orb of Regal.
currencies.notes: Common. Low value individually.

currencies.name: Orb of Alteration
currencies.effect: Rerolls the explicit modifiers on a Magic item.
currencies.primary_use: Alteration spam crafting: roll until desired mods appear, then Regal.
currencies.notes: Core crafting currency. Vendor recipe for Alts: sell identified magic item.

currencies.name: Orb of Regal
currencies.effect: Upgrades a Magic item to Rare, adding one new modifier.
currencies.primary_use: Finalising alt-spam crafted magic items; base for bench crafting.
currencies.notes: Low chaos value (~1:1 with chaos in most leagues). Often used in Archnemesis-style strategies.

currencies.name: Orb of Alchemy
currencies.effect: Upgrades a Normal item to Rare with 4–6 modifiers.
currencies.primary_use: Rolling maps (use on white maps); upgrading flasks; crafting starter gear.
currencies.notes: Extremely common. Buy ratio on trade: ~5–8 Alch = 1 Chaos.

currencies.name: Chaos Orb
currencies.effect: Rerolls all modifiers on a Rare item.
currencies.primary_use: Primary trading currency in PoE. Also used for chaos spam crafting.
currencies.notes: The baseline trade currency. Effective Chaos per hour is the main income metric.

currencies.name: Divine Orb
currencies.effect: Rerolls the numeric values of all modifiers on an item (within their ranges).
currencies.primary_use: Maximising the values of already-well-rolled items. Premium crafting currency.
currencies.notes: Primary high-end currency since 3.13 (replaced Exalted Orb in this role). ~100–200c each.

currencies.name: Exalted Orb
currencies.effect: Adds a new random modifier to a Rare item.
currencies.primary_use: Exalt slam crafting: gamble a new mod onto a near-perfect item.
currencies.notes: Once the premium currency; now ~15–25c each. Good for exalt slams. Usable in crafting bench recipes.

currencies.name: Orb of Annulment
currencies.effect: Removes one random modifier from an item.
currencies.primary_use: Removing a bad mod from a crafted item in multi-mod or suffix/prefix craft.
currencies.notes: Combined with 'Prefixes Cannot Be Changed' or 'Suffixes Cannot Be Changed' crafting for targeted removal.

currencies.name: Orb of Scouring
currencies.effect: Removes all modifiers from a Rare or Magic item, making it Normal.
currencies.primary_use: Resetting an item for a new crafting attempt.
currencies.notes: Essential for crafting reset. Cannot remove crafted (bench) mods — use Remove Crafted Mod on bench instead.

currencies.name: Blessed Orb
currencies.effect: Rerolls the numeric values of implicit modifiers on an item.
currencies.primary_use: Maximising implicit mods (rings, amulets, shields).
currencies.notes: Niche use. Most valuable on items with strong implicits like Onyx Amulets (+Attributes) or Two-Stone Rings.

currencies.name: Chromatic Orb
currencies.effect: Rerolls the colors of the sockets on an item.
currencies.primary_use: Getting desired socket colors. Biased toward the item's attribute requirements.
currencies.notes: Off-color sockets (e.g., red on an ES/Int item) require many Chromatics or crafting bench recipe.

currencies.name: Orb of Fusing
currencies.effect: Rerolls the links between sockets on an item.
currencies.primary_use: 6-linking a chest piece or weapon. Average ~1,500 Fusings for a 6-link.
currencies.notes: Vendor recipe: sell a 6-link item → 20 Fusings. Crafting bench: 6-link costs 1500 Fusings flat.

currencies.name: Jeweller's Orb
currencies.effect: Rerolls the number of sockets on an item.
currencies.primary_use: Achieving 4, 5, or 6 sockets. Bench recipe locks socket count for 350 Jewellers (6-socket).
currencies.notes: Max sockets depend on item type (body armours/2H weapons: 6; others: 4).

currencies.name: Vaal Orb
currencies.effect: Corrupts an item, applying one of four possible outcomes: random implicit, socket count/color change, quality change, or no change. Item cannot be modified after corruption.
currencies.primary_use: Adding a powerful Vaal implicit to gems or items. 6-linking items (rare).
currencies.notes: IRREVERSIBLE. Always evaluate whether the item is worth risking before using. Gems can also be corrupted: +1 level or +20% quality are top outcomes.

currencies.name: Cartographer's Chisel
currencies.effect: Increases the Quality of a Map by 1% (up to 20%).
currencies.primary_use: Chiselling maps to 20% quality before using Alchemy/Chaos for more pack size.
currencies.notes: Chisel recipe: sell a Stone Hammer/Rock Breaker/Gavel with 20% quality → 3 Chisels.

currencies.name: Awakened Sextant
currencies.effect: Adds a modifier to a Watchstone, affecting all maps in the Atlas region.
currencies.primary_use: Applying scarab-quantity, pack-size, or encounter-enabling mods to map regions.
currencies.notes: Each Sextant adds 3 uses. Stack Sextants across overlapping watchstone sockets for maximum coverage.

currencies.name: Orb of Horizons
currencies.effect: Rerolls a map into a different map of the same tier.
currencies.primary_use: Converting unwanted maps into target maps at the same tier.
currencies.notes: Useful for converting T16 clutter into your preferred T16 farming map.

currencies.name: Ancient Orb
currencies.effect: Rerolls a unique item into a different unique of the same item class.
currencies.primary_use: Gambling in the same unique class — e.g., rerolling a Praetor Crown into a Starkonja's Head.
currencies.notes: Most used in bulk for Harbinger Uniques or to hit specific league-start uniques.

currencies.name: Divine Vessel
currencies.effect: Place in the Map Device alongside a map to capture the Soul of a god when that map's boss is killed.
currencies.primary_use: Upgrading Pantheon powers in the Pantheon menu. Required to unlock major/minor god upgrades.
currencies.notes: Each captured soul upgrades one Pantheon power. Some boss souls require killing the boss on specific map layouts.

currencies.name: Enkindling Orb
currencies.effect: Rolls an enchantment on a flask that enhances its effect during use.
currencies.primary_use: Crafting powerful flask effects (e.g., 'Immune to Freeze during Flask Effect').
currencies.notes: Mutually exclusive with Instilling Orb. Enkindling = affects the 'during use' window.

currencies.name: Instilling Orb
currencies.effect: Rolls an enchantment on a flask that triggers the flask under certain conditions.
currencies.primary_use: Automating flask usage (e.g., 'Used when on Full Charges', 'Used when you take a Critical Strike').
currencies.notes: Mutually exclusive with Enkindling Orb. Instilling = triggers the flask automatically.

currencies.name: Mirror of Kalandra
currencies.effect: Creates a Mirror copy of any non-Unique, non-Corrupted item. Original item gains 'Mirrored' tag.
currencies.primary_use: Copying a perfectly-crafted item for personal use or mirror service income.
currencies.notes: Rarest drop in the game (~1 in hundreds of thousands of maps). Worth many thousands of Chaos.
